nexuscode.modelPlanner.description: Model identifier for the Planner agent. Leave empty to use the global `nexuscode.model`. Set to a stronger reasoning model (e.g. DeepSeek V4 Pro) when you want to upgrade plan quality without paying for it on every Coder invocation.

nexuscode.modelCoder.description: Model identifier for the Coder agent. Leave empty to use the global `nexuscode.model`. Set to a faster/cheaper model (e.g. DeepSeek V4 Flash) for per-step code generation when the global default is reserved for Planner.

nexuscode.modelVerifier.description: Model identifier for the Verifier agent (PRD-review LLM call). Leave empty to use the global `nexuscode.model`. Verification is short, deterministic, and tolerant of smaller/faster models — useful for cost optimization.

nexuscode.thinkingPlanner.description: V2.0: enable thinking mode for the Planner agent on Qwen 3.6 / DeepSeek R1 / similar reasoning models. When the endpoint doesn't support thinking mode, this flag is ignored (sent as opaque pass-through). Default true — the planner benefits most from explicit reasoning.

nexuscode.thinkingCoder.description: V2.0: enable thinking mode for the Coder agent. Default true. Disable if your Coder model is non-thinking and you want to skip the extra_body overhead.

nexuscode.thinkingVerifier.description: V2.0: enable thinking mode for the Verifier agent. Default true.

nexuscode.preserveThinking.description: V2.0: enable Thinking Preservation across conversation turns (Qwen 3.6 feature). Improves KV cache reuse and reduces redundant reasoning in long autonomous sessions. Has effect only when at least one role's thinking mode is enabled. Default true.

nexuscode.experimental.treeSitter.description: Use Tree-Sitter for source-code symbol extraction instead of the default regex parser. Tree-Sitter handles re-exports, dynamic imports, renamed imports, and other patterns the regex parser misses, but adds ~3.6MB of WASM grammar files to the extension bundle. Enable to validate before this becomes the default in a future release.

nexuscode.rateLimits.maxToolCallsPerTask.description: Hard cap on the total number of tool calls (read_file, write_file, bash_exec, etc.) the agent may make within a single task. Subsequent calls are rejected with an error result, preventing runaway agents from hammering the workspace. Set to 0 to reject all tool calls (debugging only). Default 100 — typical tasks complete in 10-30 calls; the cap accommodates complex multi-file edits with headroom.

nexuscode.rateLimits.perTool.description: Per-tool call limits within a single task. Map of tool name (e.g. "bash_exec", "write_file") to maximum calls. Tools not listed here are governed only by `rateLimits.maxToolCallsPerTask`. Example: {"bash_exec": 10, "write_file": 30}. Use this to constrain the most dangerous tools more tightly than the overall cap.

scripts.pretest: npm run compile && npm run lint
